tasks.goal: Collapse duplicate operator navigation into one shell-first surface with lane, approval, and queue summaries.
done_when: one primary operator entry surface is visible
workspace, approvals, and queue state are accessible without opening parallel popups

tasks.goal: Keep the shell compact and Chrome-safe by limiting concurrent panels and collapsing inactive detail views.
done_when: the shell defaults to a compact operator density
only one active detail context expands at a time

tasks.goal: Expose a bounded Neo local-runtime lane inside the Trinity editor for refresh, task staging, and current-file context handoff.
done_when: the editor shows local-runtime state without opening a separate settings surface
current-file work can be staged into Neo temp shells with a visible approval trail

tasks.goal: Seed a no-risk build queue for floor population, workflow scaffolding, and review routing.
done_when: population-ready tasks are visible in one queue view
all tasks remain approval-gated and bounded

tasks.goal: Prepare attach-only contracts for doctrine, empirical, calculator, and reference reservoir population.
done_when: source groups are attachable without rewriting originals
proof and provenance remain visible per source family

tasks.goal: Prepare a single review chamber for overlap evidence, diff packets, and promotion proposals.
done_when: review work happens in one workspace
promotion proposals route back through Smith and Trinity

tasks.goal: Prepare dataset attach controls, scenario previews, and smoke/manual render lanes for digital twin growth.
done_when: proofed datasets attach into the runtime profile
scenario preview remains bounded and reversible

tasks.goal: Keep render/runtime surfaces inside compact widget, panel, and concurrency budgets while Chrome remains responsive.
done_when: ambient and tower modes respect compact render budgets
immersive mode stays manual-only

tasks.goal: Stage health, telemetry, and evidence snapshots for each floor before deeper autonomous operation.
done_when: each resident or staged floor has a health snapshot
failures route to evidence rather than silent state

tasks.goal: Prepare Neo-led on-call and operator playbooks with Achilles oversight routed from De Sporte rather than a dedicated LightSpeed floor.
done_when: Neo has visible playbooks for intake, route, review, and publish
model usage remains bounded by host and floor policy

tasks.goal: Anchor Neo to the verified local Ollama endpoint and actual available models so low-lag buildout work can stay on-device.
done_when: Neo reports the canonical local endpoint, active profile, and available models
local-first usage stays bounded to one concurrent session by policy

tasks.goal: Create bounded temporary shells for Neo-led local assistance so floor-specific work can be staged before permanent population.
done_when: a live temp-shell registry exists on the Neo floor
each temp shell declares owner floor, model, purpose, and write scope

tasks.goal: Formalize De Sporte as a bounded co-running companion with low-sleep persistence and a compact interaction budget.
done_when: De Sporte presence is represented as a co-running contract not a competing shell
active interaction stays within the exported host/runtime budget

tasks.goal: Prepare repo, GO, and web artefact gates with explicit yes/no approval and evidence requirements.
done_when: external publish remains blocked until manual approval
handoff artefacts show source, proof, and destination
